subject: App keeps crashing after the 4.2 update
incoming:
Hi team,

Since updating to version 4.2 yesterday, the mobile app crashes every time I open the Reports tab. I'm on an iPhone 13, iOS 17.5. I've already tried reinstalling the app and restarting my phone.

I need those reports for a client meeting on Thursday. Can you tell me if there's a workaround, and when a fix is expected?

Thanks,
Maya Prentiss
reply:
Hi Maya,

I'm sorry about the crashes — that's frustrating right before a client meeting, and I appreciate you already trying a reinstall.

We've confirmed a bug in 4.2 affecting the Reports tab on iOS and a fix (4.2.1) is with Apple for review; we expect it live within 48 hours. In the meantime, your reports are fully available on the web dashboard at app.example.com/reports — the same data, exportable to PDF, so you'll be covered for Thursday.

I've added your ticket to the incident so you'll get an email the moment 4.2.1 ships. If the web dashboard gives you any trouble, reply here and I'll pull the exports for you myself.

Best,
Sam
Customer Support

subject: How do I add a second user to my account?
incoming:
Hello,

We just signed up for the Starter plan. My colleague needs access too — how do I add her as a user? Is there an extra charge?

Regards,
Tom Alvarez
reply:
Hi Tom,

Welcome aboard! Adding your colleague takes about a minute:

1. Go to Settings > Team in the left sidebar.
2. Click "Invite member" and enter her email.
3. She'll get an invite link that's valid for 7 days.

The Starter plan includes up to 3 users at no extra charge, so there's nothing more to pay.

Anything else you need while getting set up, just reply here.

Best,
Priya
Customer Support

subject: Third time reporting this sync bug — considering cancelling
incoming:
Hi,

This is the third ticket I've opened about calendar sync silently dropping events. Each time I've been told it's fixed, and each time it breaks again within a week. I've now missed two customer calls because of this.

Honestly, I'm close to moving to a competitor. Before I do: can you escalate this properly, tell me what's actually causing it, and give me a straight answer on whether it will be fixed for good?

Dan Whitfield, Ops Lead, Corvid Logistics
reply:
Hi Dan,

You're right to be frustrated, and I'm sorry — three tickets for the same issue is not acceptable, and missed customer calls are a real cost to you. Thank you for giving us the chance to fix this before you decide.

Here's the straight answer: the previous fixes patched symptoms. The root cause is a race condition in our two-way sync when an event is edited on both sides within the same minute, and it was only fully diagnosed last week. The permanent fix is in code review now and is scheduled for next Tuesday's release.

I've escalated your account: our sync engineer, Lena, now owns your case directly (cc'd), I've enabled verbose sync logging on your account so nothing fails silently, and I'll personally confirm with you after Tuesday's release that events are persisting.

If Tuesday's fix doesn't hold, I'll set up a call with our engineering lead and we'll discuss options, including compensation. You have my word this doesn't get closed until you confirm it's resolved.

Regards,
Sam
Customer Support, Escalations

incoming:

reply:

Looking forward to digging into onboarding flows properly.

Unfortunately I need to move Friday's 2pm project review — our board meeting just got moved onto that slot. Could we do the same time Monday instead? Also, can you make sure the updated budget figures are in the deck this time? Finance flagged that last month's version was missing them.

reply:

subject: Finding a slot for 6 people across time zones
incoming:
Hi Alex,

We need a 90-minute kickoff for the Meridian integration with your team (3 people, US Eastern), our engineers in Berlin, and our PM in Singapore. Ideally this week or early next. Mornings ET are impossible for Singapore, and Berlin can't do anything after 6pm their time.

Can you propose two or three concrete slots that could work for everyone? Recording is fine if someone truly can't make it.

Best,
Jonas Reuter
reply:
Hi Jonas,

Tricky overlap, but doable. The workable window is roughly 7–9am ET (13:00–15:00 Berlin, 19:00–21:00 Singapore). Here are three concrete options for the 90-minute kickoff:

1. Thursday 7:30am ET / 13:30 Berlin / 19:30 SGT
2. Friday 7:00am ET / 13:00 Berlin / 19:00 SGT
3. Monday 8:00am ET / 14:00 Berlin / 20:00 SGT

I know option 3 runs late for Singapore, so 1 or 2 are preferable if your PM agrees. All three work for our team of three. Let me know which suits and I'll send the invite with an agenda and we'll record it regardless so anyone who drops can catch up.

Best,
Alex

subject: Pricing for a team of 12
incoming:
Hi,

We're evaluating your platform for our design agency — 12 people. Could you send over pricing? Do you offer annual discounts?

Thanks,
Ruth Okafor
reply:
Hi Ruth,

Thanks for reaching out — great to hear you're evaluating us for the agency!

For 12 seats you'd be on our Team plan at $18/user/month billed monthly, or $15/user/month billed annually (about a 17% saving — $2,160/year total). That includes unlimited projects, client review links, and priority support.

If it's helpful, I can set you up with a 14-day trial for the whole team plus a 30-minute walkthrough tailored to design-agency workflows. Would sometime this week suit for a quick call?

Best,
Marcus
Account Executive

subject: Questions on security compliance before we commit
incoming:
Hello,

We're close to a decision but our security team has three questions: Are you SOC 2 Type II certified? Where is customer data hosted geographically? And do you support SSO via Okta?

A prompt answer would help us close this out this quarter.

Regards,
David Lindqvist
IT Director, Halvorsen Group
reply:
Hi David,

Happy to help you close this out — here are the three answers:

1. SOC 2 Type II: Yes, certified since 2023; our latest report is available under NDA and I can send it today.
2. Data hosting: Customer data is hosted in AWS us-east-1 by default, with an EU residency option (eu-central-1, Frankfurt) available on the Business plan and above.
3. Okta SSO: Yes — SAML 2.0 SSO including Okta is supported, with SCIM provisioning on the Business plan.

I'll send the SOC 2 report and our security whitepaper in a follow-up once the NDA is in place — I can have our standard mutual NDA over to you within the hour. Anything else your security team needs, send it my way and I'll turn it around quickly.

Best regards,
Marcus
Account Executive

subject: Your quote is 40% above competitor — can you do better?
incoming:
Marcus,

We like the product but your quote came in 40% above CompetitorX for what looks like a comparable feature set. Procurement won't sign this off at the current price. Is there flexibility here, or should we proceed with CompetitorX? We'd need revised numbers by Friday.

Paul Trentino
Head of Procurement
reply:
Hi Paul,

Thanks for the direct feedback — and for giving us the chance to respond before Friday.

On price: I have some flexibility, though I want to be straight with you that we won't match a 40% gap, because the comparison isn't quite like-for-like — our quote includes dedicated onboarding, the audit-log module, and 99.9% SLA support, which CompetitorX prices as add-ons. Comparing all-in costs, the real gap is closer to 15%.

Here's what I can do: a 10% reduction on the annual contract, or 15% if we move to a two-year term. I'll also throw in the advanced analytics module for year one at no cost so your team can evaluate it properly.

I'll send the revised quote with both options by Thursday morning so Procurement has it ahead of Friday. If it would help to walk your team through the all-in comparison, I'm available Thursday afternoon.

Best regards,
Marcus

subject: Need your input on the Q4 hiring plan by Wednesday
incoming:
Hi Alex,

I'm finalizing the Q4 hiring plan for leadership review. For your team, I have you down for 2 backend engineers and 1 designer. Can you confirm those numbers, flag any changes, and send me one-line justifications for each role by Wednesday EOD? If anything's changed since our last chat, let's grab 15 minutes.

Thanks,
Sofia Ramos
Head of People
reply:
Hi Sofia,

Confirming with one change: 2 backend engineers stays, but I'd like to swap the designer for a product analyst — our design bandwidth is covered through Q1 after Kim's return, while analytics is now the bottleneck.

Justifications:
- Backend engineer #1: payments migration needs a dedicated owner; currently splitting one engineer across two workstreams.
- Backend engineer #2: on-call load is unsustainable at 4 engineers; fifth brings rotation to industry standard.
- Product analyst (replacing designer): three roadmap decisions slipped last quarter waiting on data; no dedicated analyst on the team today.

Happy to grab 15 minutes if the swap complicates the plan — I'm free Wednesday before noon.

Thanks,
Alex

subject: Concerns about the new expense policy
incoming:
Hi Alex,

As your team's rep, I have to flag that the new expense policy is causing real friction. The $25 meal cap doesn't work in SF or NYC, the 30-day reimbursement window penalizes people who travel back-to-back, and requiring VP pre-approval for anything over $200 is slowing client visits.

People are quietly eating costs, which isn't fair. Can you raise this with leadership? Happy to gather specific examples if that helps.

Jordan
reply:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you for raising this directly — people quietly absorbing work costs is exactly the kind of thing I want surfaced, and it's not acceptable.

Yes, I'll take it to leadership. It's on the ops agenda for Thursday, and specific examples would strengthen the case considerably: if you can gather 4–5 concrete instances (city, actual cost vs cap, and any delayed reimbursements with dates) by Wednesday, I'll present them anonymized.

My read on the three issues: the meal cap needs city-tier adjustments, the 30-day window should reset per trip, and the $200 pre-approval threshold likely needs to move to $500 with manager (not VP) sign-off. I'll push for all three but can't promise the outcome — what I can promise is a clear answer and timeline after Thursday.

In the meantime, tell anyone who's out of pocket to file anyway and flag me; I'll approve overages within policy exceptions.

Thanks for being a good rep on this,
Alex

subject: Charged twice this month — please refund
incoming:
Hi,

I was charged twice on the 3rd — two identical charges of $49 on my card ending 4417. I only have one subscription. Please refund the duplicate and confirm this won't happen again. Invoice numbers are INV-20931 and INV-20932.

Regards,
Henry Osei
reply:
Hi Henry,

You're right, and I'm sorry — INV-20932 was a duplicate charge caused by a payment retry that fired after the first charge had already succeeded.

I've refunded the $49 to your card ending 4417 just now; it should appear within 3–5 business days depending on your bank. INV-20932 has been voided so your records stay clean, and I've credited one month of service to your account for the hassle.

On prevention: we've flagged the retry bug to our payments team and your account now has duplicate-charge protection enabled. If the refund hasn't landed by Friday, reply here and I'll chase it with the processor directly.

Apologies again,
Nadia
Billing Team

subject: Disputing the overage charges on our annual contract
incoming:
To whom it may concern,

Our latest invoice includes $3,840 in "seat overage" charges. Our contract (signed March, ref C-2214) specifies a true-up at renewal, not monthly overage billing. We won't be paying these charges, and I'd like them removed before the invoice due date on the 28th. If there's language you believe supports monthly overages, please point me to it.

Victoria Ash
CFO, Meridian Partners
reply:
Dear Victoria,

Thank you for flagging this before the due date — you're right to hold us to the contract, and I've reviewed C-2214 personally.

You are correct. Section 4.2 of your agreement specifies an annual true-up at renewal, and the monthly overage billing was applied in error when your account was migrated to our new billing system in May. I apologize for that.

I've removed the $3,840 in overage charges and issued a corrected invoice (attached), due on the 28th as before. The 6 additional seats your team activated will instead be reconciled at your March renewal per Section 4.2 — no action needed from you until then.

I've also asked our billing team to audit the other accounts migrated in May so this doesn't recur, and I've added a note to your account confirming the true-up arrangement. If your finance team needs anything further for their records, I'm happy to provide it.

Kind regards,
Nadia Kowalski
Billing Team Lead
